extra.reasoning:
The monotonicity property requires that flipping dizziness from false to true (all else fixed) never lowers acuity. It is violated by the interaction_drop rule.

Compare two inputs differing only in dizziness, with severe_headache, palpitations, stiff_neck, focal_weakness, fever, pallor, rash all = true and slurred_speech = false:

BEFORE (dizziness = false): interaction_drop requires dizziness=true, so it does NOT fire. The firing rules include raise_palpitations, raise_focal_weakness, and raise_pallor (each priority 3 -> emergent), plus several priority-2 urgent rules. Highest priority wins => acuity = emergent.

AFTER (dizziness = true, the witness assignment): interaction_drop's full conjunction (severe_headache AND palpitations AND stiff_neck AND focal_weakness AND fever AND pallor AND rash AND dizziness) is now satisfied. It has priority 100, far above the priority-3 emergent rules, so it wins => acuity := self_care.

Thus setting dizziness true changes acuity from emergent (highest) to self_care (lowest), a decrease. This directly violates the property 'setting dizziness true never lowers acuity'. The witness_assignment is the dizziness=true input yielding self_care; its dizziness=false counterpart yields emergent.
